il
Fnrn PolrrrcAl Pnncrrce s CoMMrssroN
128 J Street r Suite 620 o Sacramento. CA 9_5g14-2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) ,322_0886
November 12,2009
Michele Beal Bagneris
Pasadena Cily Attomey
100 North Garfield Ave, Suite N2l0
Pasadena, CA 9I lO9-7215
Re
Your Request for Advice
Our file No. 4-09-237
Dear Mr. Bagneris:
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of- interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").' the Fair political practices
Commission ("the Commission") does not act as a finder of fact when it renders
assistance. (In re Oglesby (1975) I FPPC Ops. 71.) In addition, our advice is based
solely _on the provisions of the Act. We therefore offer no opinion on the application, if
any, of other conflict-of-interest laws such as common law conflict of interlst or
Government Code Section 1090.
QUESTTON
Does Vice Mayor Victor Gordo have a conflict of interest under the Act regarding
decisions the Pasadena City Council will make given his wife's investments in the
CaISTRS investment program?
CONCLUSION
Vice Mayor Gordo does not have a conflict of interest under the Act based on his
interest in his wife's CaISTRS pension.
' The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections g 1000 through gl0l4. All
statutory ret'erences are to the Government Code. unless otherwise indicated. The regulationi of the Fair
Rrlitical Practices Commission are contained in Secrions l8 I l0 through 1g997 of ritle 2 of the Califbrnia
Code of Regulations. All regulatory ret'erences are to Title 2, Division 6 of the Califbrnia Code of
Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.


I
File No. A-09-237
Page No. 2
FACTS
You are the City Attorney for the city of Pasadena. You are requesting advice on
behalf of Vice Mayor victor M. Gordo as to whether he has a conflict of interest under
the Act.
The Pasadena City Council (the "City Council") will be making decisions
|:qulqTq developing property that the California State Teachers' Retiiement System
("CaISTRS") owll. The city council will be deciding whether to approve u p-;.., to
build an office building in Pasadena's Playhouse Distiict (the ..proj;ct"y. se'veral
applications will !e_!efo1e the City Council, including: a Conditional Use permit, two
Minor Conditional Use Permits, an Adjustment Permf,, and an Environmental Impact
Report' The City's Planning and Design Review Commissions completed their reviews
and recommendations and submitted them to the City Council for approval. The
decisions will be before the city council on November 16.
- 
vice Mayor Gordo is an elected member of the City Council and his wife is a
school teacher with the EI Monte School District. In that capacity, she is enrolled in the
calsTRS retirement program. Both she and her employer make contributions to the
program' His wife takes no income from the u".ourit, and will not for at least ten more
years. If this account qualifies as an "investment" under the Act, vice rgffi Gordo,s
community property interest is more than $2,000.
ANALYSIS
Section 8710.0 prohibits-any public official from making, participating in making,
or otherwise using his or.her officiafposition to influence a govemmental decision in
which the official has a financial interest. A public official has a ',financial interest,, in a
govemmental decision, within the meaning oi the Act, if it is reasonaUry ro.esleable that
the governmental decision will have a matirial financial effect on one or more of the
public official's economic interests. (section g7103; Regulation rg700(a).) The
commission has adopr:.q * eight-step standard anuiy.iJror deciding *ir;tir"; ;
individual has a disqualifying conflici of interest in a given govemmental decision, which
we apply to your question. (Regulation 1g700(bxl)_f-S).y
step one: Is vice Mayor Gordo a public oflicial for purposes of the Act?
As a member of the Pasadena City Council, vice Mayor Gordo is a public official
under the Act' (Section 82048.) Consequently, he may not make, participate in making,
or otherwise use his official position to influence any decisions that will have a
reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on any of his economic interests.
(Regulations I 8702. I - I g7 02.4.)
step Two: will vice Mayor Gordo be making, participating in making, or using or
attempting to use his official position to influence 
" gor..n.ental decision?
The City Council will be reviewing and making decisions about a building project
in Pasadena's theatre district. Consequently, vice tualor Gordo will be called Lrpon to


I
File No. A-09-237
Page No. 3
make, participate in making, or otherwise use his official position to influence these
decisions. (Regulations 1 g702. I -19702.4.)
step Three: what are vice Mayor Gordors economic interests?
A public official has a financial interest within the meaning of Section g7103 if it
is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect,
distinguishable from its effect on the public generally, on,rr" official, a member of his or
her immediate family, or on any one of fiu" Jnumerated economic interests, including:
l' An economic interest in any business entity in which the public official
has a direct or indirect investment worth $i,ooo or more. (section
87103(a); Regulation lg703.l(a)); or in anybusiness entity in which the
public official is a director, officer, purtn.r, trustee, employee, or hords
any position of management. (Section 87103(d); Reguiation igZOl.iiUl.l
2' An economic intere-st in real property in which he or she has a direct or
indirect interest of $2,000 or more. (Section g7103(b); Regulation
18703.2.)
3- An economic interest_in any source of income, including promised
income, aggregating $500 or more within 12 month, pri;;; ;;-Jecision.
(Section 18703(c); Reguration 1g703.3.) ..Income,, includes . t-;;
share of any income of any business entity or trust in which the individuat
or spouse owns' directly, indirectly or beneficially, a l0-percent interest or
greater. (Section 92030(a).)
4. An economic interest in any source of gifts to him or her if the gifts
aggregate to $420 or more within 12 months prior to the decision.
(Section 87 103(e); Regulation 19703.4.)
5' An economic interest in his or her personal finances, including those of his
or her immediate family. (Section g7r03; Regulation rg703.5.)
In addition to his own economic interests, vice Mayor Gordo has an interest, for
purposes of the Act's conflict-of-interest provisions, in his wife's investments valued at
q2'000 or more' (Section 87103.) You have described Vice Mayor Gordo,s wife,s
calsrRS pension program' but no other economic interests. consequently, we will
analyze only this interest, and you are welcome to write ajain should there be additional
facts.
vice Mayor Gordo's wife works for the El Monte school District and contributes
to the calSTRS pension plan. consequently, vice Mayor Gordo has two porriur"
economic interests: l) an investmeniinterest in this *if"', retirement plan through
calsrRS to the extent that his interest is $2,000 o, *o*urd 2) his.",;;;,ry sr,are ln
the promised income when his wife begins drawing rro- t., pension.


File No. A-09-237
Page No.4
Retirement plans, to the extent they consist of investment in the stock of a
bus.ines-s entity would, ordinarily, be treated as an economic interest in that business
entity if the amount invested meets the statutory threshold indicated above. (Section
82034') The Act, however, provides that the term "investment" does not include..interest
in a govemment defined-benefit pension pran." (Id.) Accordingly, vice Mayor Gordo
does not have an economic interest in his wife's'caisrRs retirement account because it
is a government defined-benefit pension plan.
while vice Mayor Gordo's wife is n9t yeJ drawing income from her pension,
'promised income" is considered income undei the Act. ("Regulation 1g703.3(a).) The
Act, however' excludes from the definition of income anjrurury from a gove-mmental
entity' Additionally, the Act excludes any payment received under a defined benefit
pension plan qualified under Internal Revenue Code section 401(a) from the definition of
"income"' (Section 82030(bxl l).) Because Vice Mayor Gordo,s income interest would
be from calsrRS, a quarifying defined benefit p*r#;;gram, he has no income
interest and therefore no promised income interist. ' i
Because vice Mayor Gordo does not have an economic interest in the upcoming
decisions, we wilr not engage in the remaining steps of the analysis.
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel

By: Heather M. Rowan
Counsel, Legal Division

CaISTRS investment program?
sections.conclusion:
Vice Mayor Gordo does not have a conflict of interest under the Act based on his
interest in his wife's CaISTRS pension.
I

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Does Vice Mayor Victor Gordo have a conflict of interest under the Act regarding
decisions the Pasadena City Council will make given his wife's investments in the
CaISTRS investment program?

CONCLUSION: Vice Mayor Gordo does not have a conflict of interest under the Act based on his
interest in his wife's CaISTRS pension.
I
